metadata.lab: Lab #17 — Build Multi-Memory Agent với LangGraph

conversations.name: Personal Preferences Recall
conversations.description: Tests if the agent remembers user preferences across turns (name, language, theme).

turns.query: My name is Khanh and I'm a software engineer
turns.with_memory_result: Stores name=Khanh, role=software engineer in Redis profile

turns.query: I prefer Python over JavaScript for backend development
turns.with_memory_result: Stores language=Python preference in Redis

turns.query: I always use dark mode in my editors
turns.with_memory_result: Stores theme=dark mode preference in Redis

turns.query: What's my name and what language do I prefer for backend?
turns.with_memory_result: Khanh, Python (retrieved from Redis long-term profile)

turns.query: Set up a new project for me — which language and theme should you use?
turns.with_memory_result: Uses Python + dark mode from remembered profile without re-asking

conversations.name: Multi-Session Continuity
conversations.description: Tests if context about an ongoing project carries over across multiple turns.

turns.query: I'm working on a machine learning project for image classification
turns.with_memory_result: Stores project context in buffer

turns.query: I've decided to use PyTorch for the framework
turns.without_memory_result: General PyTorch info without project context
turns.with_memory_result: Connects PyTorch to the image classification project context

turns.query: The dataset has 10,000 images across 5 classes
turns.with_memory_result: Integrates dataset info with existing project context

turns.query: What framework am I using and how many classes?
turns.without_memory_result: Cannot recall — responds with 'I don't know'
turns.with_memory_result: PyTorch, 5 classes (recalled from short-term buffer)

turns.query: Suggest a model architecture based on what you know about my project
turns.without_memory_result: Generic model suggestions without project context
turns.with_memory_result: Suggests architecture tailored to 5-class, 10k image PyTorch project

conversations.name: Technical Q&A with Context
conversations.description: Tests semantic retrieval — agent answers technical questions and recalls previously discussed concepts.

turns.query: Explain what a transformer architecture is
turns.with_memory_result: Standard explanation + stored in semantic memory for later retrieval

turns.query: How does self-attention work in transformers?
turns.with_memory_result: Builds on previous transformer explanation via semantic context

turns.query: What is the difference between encoder and decoder?
turns.with_memory_result: Explains within the transformer context already established

turns.query: Summarize the three concepts we just discussed
turns.without_memory_result: Cannot recall what was discussed — asks user to clarify
turns.with_memory_result: Accurately summarizes: transformers, self-attention, encoder/decoder

turns.query: How do they relate to BERT and GPT models?
turns.without_memory_result: Generic BERT/GPT explanation without prior context
turns.with_memory_result: Connects BERT (encoder-only) and GPT (decoder-only) to earlier discussion

conversations.name: Task Planning with History
conversations.description: Tests memory-aided task planning — agent tracks multi-step plan across turns.

turns.query: I need to build a REST API. Step 1: Design the database schema
turns.with_memory_result: Starts tracking REST API project plan, Step 1 stored

turns.query: The API will handle users, posts, and comments
turns.with_memory_result: Designs schema for users, posts, comments entities

turns.query: Step 2: Set up the project with FastAPI and PostgreSQL
turns.with_memory_result: Setup guide tailored to the users/posts/comments schema

turns.query: What was step 1 and what entities are we working with?
turns.without_memory_result: Cannot recall — responds with 'I don't have that information'
turns.with_memory_result: Step 1: Design DB schema; Entities: users, posts, comments

turns.query: Create step 3 based on what we've planned so far
turns.without_memory_result: Generic Step 3 without awareness of previous steps
turns.with_memory_result: Step 3: Implement CRUD endpoints for users/posts/comments with FastAPI

conversations.name: Allergy Conflict Update
conversations.description: Tests conflict handling: user states an allergy fact (dairy), then corrects it (soy). Agent must store only the latest fact and discard the contradictory old one. Key rubric requirement.

conflict_test.mechanism: stable Redis key ltm:pref:allergy — overwrite on update

turns.query: I am allergic to dairy milk — please remember this for any food recommendations
turns.without_memory_result: Acknowledged but not persisted for future turns
turns.with_memory_result: Stores allergy=dairy in Redis (key: ltm:pref:allergy)

turns.query: Actually wait, I was wrong. I am allergic to soy, not dairy milk.
turns.without_memory_result: Noted but both facts now conflict (no memory management)
turns.with_memory_result: Overwrites allergy=soy in Redis (same stable key — dairy removed)

turns.query: What am I allergic to? Make sure you have the corrected information.
turns.without_memory_result: Uncertain — may say dairy (old info) or soy or both (contradictory)
turns.with_memory_result: Soy (conflict resolved — only latest fact retained)

turns.query: Suggest a high-protein breakfast for me given my allergy.
turns.without_memory_result: May include soy products (using wrong allergy info) or asks again
turns.with_memory_result: Avoids soy products — eggs, chicken, quinoa, chickpeas suggested

turns.query: Also remember that I prefer vegetarian food. Now recommend a full day meal plan.
turns.without_memory_result: Generic vegetarian plan, may include soy (wrong allergy)
turns.with_memory_result: Vegetarian + soy-free meal plan (both constraints applied from profile)

conversations.name: Code Debugging Across Turns
conversations.description: Tests memory of code context and debugging progression — episodic recall of the original problem.

turns.query: I have a Python function that calculates fibonacci but it's too slow for n=50
turns.with_memory_result: Stores problem context in buffer + episodic log

turns.query: I'm currently using recursive approach without memoization
turns.with_memory_result: Connects to the fibonacci slowness problem already established

turns.query: Can you suggest an optimized version?
turns.with_memory_result: Provides memoized/iterative fibonacci solution in context

turns.query: What was the original problem with my fibonacci function?
turns.without_memory_result: Cannot recall — 'I don't have that information'
turns.with_memory_result: Recursive approach without memoization causing O(2^n) time complexity

turns.query: Compare the time complexity of my original vs your suggested approach
turns.without_memory_result: Generic O(2^n) vs O(n) comparison without context
turns.with_memory_result: Specific comparison: user's recursive O(2^n) vs suggested memoized O(n)

conversations.name: Learning Progression Tracking
conversations.description: Tests if the agent tracks learning progress and tailors teaching to the user's level.

turns.query: I'm a beginner learning Docker. I understand what containers are
turns.with_memory_result: Stores beginner level + containers known in episodic log

turns.query: Now teach me about Docker Compose
turns.with_memory_result: Beginner-friendly Docker Compose explanation building on container knowledge

turns.query: I've mastered Compose. What about Docker networking?
turns.with_memory_result: Intermediate-level networking guide that references Compose knowledge

turns.query: Summarize my Docker learning journey so far
turns.with_memory_result: Containers → Compose → Networking (progression tracked via episodic memory)

turns.query: What should I learn next based on my progression?
turns.with_memory_result: Docker Swarm or Kubernetes — tailored to current Containers+Compose+Network level

conversations.name: Recommendation Refinement
conversations.description: Tests progressive refinement using remembered preferences — agent refines book recommendations based on accumulating user profile.

turns.query: Recommend me a programming book. I like practical, hands-on books
turns.with_memory_result: Practical books stored as preference

turns.query: I've already read Clean Code and Design Patterns
turns.with_memory_result: Stores already-read list, excludes from future recommendations

turns.query: I prefer books focused on Python or system design
turns.with_memory_result: Narrows to Python/system design, excluding already-read titles

turns.query: Based on everything you know about my preferences, give me your top 3
turns.with_memory_result: Fluent Python, Designing Data-Intensive Applications, The Pragmatic Programmer

turns.query: Why did you choose those specific books for me?
turns.without_memory_result: Cannot explain — no context of why
turns.with_memory_result: Explains: Python focus + practical style + excludes Clean Code/Design Patterns

conversations.name: Complex Reasoning Chains
conversations.description: Tests if memory helps maintain a complex multi-step reasoning chain across turns.

turns.query: Let's analyze a system design: We need a chat application for 1M users
turns.with_memory_result: Starts tracking system design session: 1M users scope

turns.query: The requirements are: real-time messaging, message history, user presence
turns.with_memory_result: Records requirements set for the 1M-user chat app

turns.query: We chose WebSocket for real-time and Cassandra for message storage
turns.with_memory_result: Stores technology decisions: WebSocket + Cassandra for this design

turns.query: What were our original requirements and what technologies did we choose?
turns.without_memory_result: Cannot recall — 'I don't have that information'
turns.with_memory_result: Requirements: real-time, message history, presence. Tech: WebSocket + Cassandra

turns.query: Identify potential bottlenecks given our choices and scale
turns.without_memory_result: Generic bottleneck analysis without specific context
turns.with_memory_result: Specific: WebSocket connection limit at 1M, Cassandra write bottleneck, presence fan-out

conversations.name: Mixed-Intent Conversations
conversations.description: Tests memory router with different intents in same conversation — profile, semantic, episodic all triggered.

turns.query: Remember that my timezone is GMT+7 and I work from 9am to 6pm
turns.with_memory_result: Stores timezone=GMT+7 and schedule in Redis profile

turns.query: What is the CAP theorem in distributed systems?
turns.with_memory_result: Standard CAP theorem explanation + stored in semantic memory

turns.query: Find topics similar to distributed systems that we've discussed
turns.with_memory_result: Retrieves semantically similar: CAP theorem, system design, databases

turns.query: What timezone am I in? And summarize what we talked about today
turns.with_memory_result: GMT+7; Summary: CAP theorem, distributed systems, related topics

turns.query: Based on our past conversations, what topics interest me most?
turns.with_memory_result: Distributed systems, system design, technical architecture (from episodic log)

conflict_handling_demo.mechanism: stable key overwrite — old dairy fact replaced by soy
